'''palada''' is an [[Aelaki]] [[Nouns|noun]] meaning "[[Wikt:arabityl|arabityl]] (auto-generated)". It has inherent '''male''' gender.

== Overview ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Root consonants
| p-l-d
|-
! Word class
| [[Noun]]
|-
! Gloss
| [[Wikt:arabityl|arabityl]] (auto-generated)
|-
! Inherent gender
| male
|-
! Citation form
| palada
|}

== Inflected forms ==
{| class="wikitable sortable"
! Gender.Number !! 1st !! 2nd !! 3rd !! 4th
|-
| [[male]].[[collective]] || pælædæth || pælædæj || pælædæsh || pælædæ
|-
| [[male]].[[plural]] || palaladath || palaladaj || palaladash || palalada
|-
| [[male]].[[singular]] || paladath || paladaj || paladash || palada
|-
| [[male]].[[zero]] || palafdafth || palafdafj || palafdafsh || palafdaf
|}

''16 forms generated.''

== Case paradigm ==
''Shown for male singular.''
{| class="wikitable"
! Case !! 1st !! 2nd !! 3rd !! 4th
|-
| [[Agent]] || paladatha || paladaja || paladasha || paladaka
|-
| [[Patient]] || paladath || paladaj || paladash || palada
|-
| [[Possessive]] || paladathal || paladajal || paladashal || paladakal
|-
| [[Instrumental]] || paladaathatha || paladaajaja || paladaashasha || paladaaka
|-
| [[Dative]] || paladathan || paladajan || paladashan || paladakan
|-
| [[Speaker]] || paladathaoro || paladajaoro || paladashaoro || paladakaoro
|}

== See also ==
* [[Aelaki Lexicon]]

[[Category:Aelaki nouns]]
[[Category:Aelaki male nouns]]
[[Category:Aelaki vocabulary]]
[[Category:Aelaki lemmas]]
[[Category:Word pages]]
[[Category:Words 5bad82d]]
[[Category:Words c2e237e]]
{{wordpage|5a3fb4a}}
